name: CT Angiography Abdomen
description: CT angiography of the abdominal aorta and visceral vessels

findings.technique: CT angiography of the abdomen was performed with intravenous contrast from the diaphragmatic dome through the femoral arteries.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Abdominal Aorta
sections.content: The abdominal aorta measures [X] cm in maximal diameter and demonstrates [no aneurysm / no dissection / no ulceration]. No atherosclerotic plaque causing significant stenosis.

sections.name: Celiac Artery
sections.content: The celiac artery and its branches (splenic, common hepatic, left gastric) are [patent / no stenosis / no aneurysm].

sections.name: Superior Mesenteric Artery
sections.content: The SMA is [patent / no stenosis / no dissection]. The branch vessels are [normal / patent].

sections.name: Inferior Mesenteric Artery
sections.content: The IMA is [patent / no stenosis / no occlusion]. No ischemic bowel.

sections.name: Renal Arteries
sections.content: The bilateral renal arteries demonstrate [no stenosis / no aneurysm]. The kidneys are [perfused / no infarction].

sections.name: Iliac Arteries
sections.content: The bilateral common iliac arteries are [patent / no stenosis / no aneurysm]. The external iliac arteries are [patent].

impression:
1. Normal CT angiography of the abdomen.
2. No aneurysm, dissection, or significant stenosis of the abdominal aorta or visceral vessels.
author: RadReport Templates
